ddls:
CREATE TABLE "surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey */,
  "survey_name" TEXT /* Name of the survey */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the survey */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the survey (e.g., political affiliation, economic satisfaction) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the survey (e.g., active, closed, draft) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the survey */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the survey */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the survey */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the survey */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "questions" (
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each question */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey to which the question belongs */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic category to which the question belongs */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the question type */,
  "format_id" INTEGER /* ID of the response format */,
  "question_text" TEXT /* Text of the question being asked */,
  "question_type" TEXT /* Type of the question (e.g., discrete, open-ended) */,
  "response_format" TEXT /* Format of the response (e.g., numeric, text) */,
  "required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the question is mandatory */,
  "order" INTEGER /* Order of the question in the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("question_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_questions_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_questions_format_id FOREIGN KEY ("format_id") REFERENCES response_formats ("format_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_questions_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES question_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_questions_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "question_id" INTEGER /* ID of the question being answered */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant providing the response */,
  "response_value" REAL /* Value of the response given by the participant */,
  "valid" INTEGER /* Indicates if the response is valid (true/false) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was submitted */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to submit the response */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the participant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_question_id FOREIGN KEY ("question_id") REFERENCES questions ("question_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the participant */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the participant */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the participant */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the participant */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the participant */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest educational level attained by the participant */,
  "income_bracket" TEXT /* Income bracket of the participant */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the participant */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the participant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_validations" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation entry */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the response being validated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Indicates if the response is valid */,
  "invalid_reason" TEXT /* Reason for invalidation, if applicable */,
  "validated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who validated the response */,
  "validated_at" TEXT /* Date and time of validation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_validations_validated_by FOREIGN KEY ("validated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_validations_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the demographic category (e.g., Age, Gender) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the demographic category */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the category is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the category */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "question_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each question type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the question type (e.g., open-ended, discrete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the question type */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the type is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the type */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_formats" (
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response format */,
  "format_name" TEXT /* Name of the response format (e.g., numeric, text) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the response format */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the format is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the format */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("format_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the survey */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser) used to access the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the user account */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
